content.title: Metric Space
content.content:
A **metric space** is a pair $(X, d)$ where $X$ 
is a set and $d: X \times X \to [0, \infty)$ is a function (called a 
**metric**) satisfying:

1. **Positive definiteness:** $d(x, y) = 0$ if and only if $x = y$.

2. **Symmetry:** $d(x, y) = d(y, x)$ for all $x, y \in X$.

3. **Triangle inequality:** $d(x, z) \leq d(x, y) + d(y, z)$ for 
all $x, y, z \in X$.

content.title: Standard Metrics
content.problem:
1. $\mathbb{R}$ with $d(x, y) = |x - y|$.

2. $\mathbb{R}^n$ with Euclidean distance: $d(x, y) = \sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - y_i)^2}$.

3. $\mathbb{R}^n$ with taxicab distance: $d_1(x, y) = \sum_{i=1}^n |x_i - y_i|$.

4. $\mathbb{R}^n$ with max distance: $d_\infty(x, y) = \max_i |x_i - y_i|$.

5. $C[a, b]$ with supremum norm: $d(f, g) = \sup_{x \in [a,b]} |f(x) - g(x)|$.

6. Any set $X$ with discrete metric: $d(x, y) = 0$ if $x = y$, $d(x, y) = 1$ otherwise.

content.text: All topological concepts carry over from $\mathbb{R}$:

content.title: Open Ball
content.content:
The **open ball** of radius $r > 0$ centered at $x$ is
\[
B_r(x) = \{y \in X : d(x, y) < r\}.
\]

content.title: Open and Closed Sets
content.content:
A set $O \subseteq X$ is **open** if for each $x \in O$, there exists 
$r > 0$ with $B_r(x) \subseteq O$.

A set $F \subseteq X$ is **closed** if its complement is open.

content.title: Convergence
content.content:
A sequence $(x_n)$ in $(X, d)$ **converges** to $x$ if 
$d(x_n, x) \to 0$ as $n \to \infty$.

content.title: Continuity
content.content:
$f: (X, d_X) \to (Y, d_Y)$ is **continuous** at $x$ if for every 
$\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $\delta > 0$ such that 
$d_X(x', x) < \delta \implies d_Y(f(x'), f(x)) < \varepsilon$.

content.title: Sequential Characterizations
content.content:
In metric spaces:

1. $F$ is closed iff whenever $x_n \in F$ and $x_n \to x$, then $x \in F$.

2. $f$ is continuous iff $x_n \to x$ implies $f(x_n) \to f(x)$.
content.label: Theorem
